skill.name: libtv-skill
skill.description: agent-im 会话技能 - 通过 liblib.tv 的 AI 能力生成和编辑图片/视频。覆盖场景包括：生成（文生图、文生视频、图生视频、做动画、画一个 xxx、来段 xxx）、编辑修改（把 xxx 换成 yyy、去掉 xxx、加上 xxx、改成 xxx、调整 xxx、局部修改、改镜头）、风格转换（风格迁移、转绘、换风格）、视频续写延长、复刻视频/TVC/宣传片、短剧/短漫剧生成、音乐 MV 生成、产品广告/展示片制作、分镜/故事板设计、教育视频/短视频制作。当用户提到 liblib、libtv、上传参考图/视频、查看生成进度时也应触发。关键判断：只要用户的请求涉及 AI 图片或视频的创作、生成、编辑、修改，无论措辞如何（如"画只猫"、"做个海报"、"把纸船换成爱心"、"这个视频帮我改一下"、"帮我复刻这段视频"、"用这首歌做个 MV"、"一句话生成短剧"），都必须触发此技能。
skill.summary: AI 图片/视频生成与编辑技能，通过 liblib.tv API 实现文生图、文生视频、图生视频、编辑修改、风格转换等创作功能。
skill.author: libtv-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 119 patterns across 7 files, but all are false positives upon evaluation. Network requests target only liblib.tv official API endpoints. Environment access is limited to the skill's own API key configuration. File operations only write downloaded content to user-specified directories. The Ruby/shell backtick detections in SKILL.md are Markdown code fences in documentation, not actual command execution. No cryptographic functions, credential exfiltration, or malicious patterns exist. This is a legitimate API client skill for AI media generation.

content.user_title: Generate and Edit AI Images and Videos
content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated images and videos typically requires navigating complex platforms and writing detailed prompts. This skill connects directly to liblib.tv's API, letting you generate and edit visual content through natural language commands.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text descriptions using models like Seedance 2.0, Kling 3.0, Wan 2.6, NanoBanana, Midjourney, and Seedream 5.0
Generate videos from text prompts or reference images (text-to-video, image-to-video)
Edit existing images and videos by replacing elements, adjusting styles, or modifying specific regions
Upload reference images and videos (up to 200MB) to the liblib.tv OSS for use in generation tasks
Poll generation progress and automatically download completed results to local directories
Create and manage conversation sessions for multi-turn creative workflows
limitations: Requires a valid LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY environment variable for API authentication
Generation tasks for complex outputs like short dramas or MVs may take several minutes to complete
Only supports image and video file uploads; other file types are rejected
Polling timeout is set to 3 minutes; longer tasks require manual checking via the project canvas link

use_cases.title: Content Creator Generating Storyboards
use_cases.description: A video creator uses natural language to generate a 9-panel storyboard for their short film project, then downloads the results for reference during shooting.

use_cases.title: Marketer Creating Product Advertisement
use_cases.description: A marketing professional uploads a product photo and requests an AI-generated promotional video with specific styling, receiving the final output for campaign use.

use_cases.title: Educator Making Tutorial Videos
use_cases.description: An educator generates animated explainer videos from text descriptions to enhance their online course materials without needing video editing skills.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a sunset over mountains with a lake in the foreground, anime style
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Video Generation with Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 10-second video of a paper boat floating on water, using this reference image for style: {upload_file first}
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to generate video with a specific visual style from a reference image

prompt_templates.title: Element Replacement Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace all the paper boats in this video with white paper hearts: {upload_file first}
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to edit an existing video by replacing specific elements

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Scene Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete short drama with 16:9 aspect ratio, nine-panel storyboard layout showing the confrontation, conflict, crisis, and resolution scenes
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a complex multi-scene narrative requiring backend workflow orchestration

output_examples.input: Create an anime video of a cat playing piano
output_examples.output: Session created successfully. Project URL: https://www.liblib.tv/canvas?projectId=xxx. Generation in progress. Polling for results...

output_examples.input: Download all results from session abc-123
output_examples.output: Downloaded 9 files to ~/Downloads/libtv_results: 01.png through 09.png. Total: 9 files.

best_practices: Always upload reference files before sending edit commands that require them
Use polling with incremental sequence numbers (--after-seq) to efficiently track generation progress
Provide the project canvas URL to users only after generation completes, not during the process
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite, expand, or polish user prompts - send them exactly as the user provided to the backend AI
Do not split a single generation request into multiple separate calls unless explicitly requested
Do not add your own creative descriptions or style modifiers to user prompts

faq.question: What API key do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY from liblib.tv. Set it as an environment variable before using the skill.

faq.question: How long does generation take?
faq.answer: Simple images may complete in under a minute. Complex videos, short dramas, or MVs can take several minutes. The skill polls every 8 seconds and stops after 3 minutes if incomplete.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Only images (image/*) and videos (video/*) are supported, with a maximum file size of 200MB.

faq.question: Where are downloaded files saved?
faq.answer: By default, files are saved to ~/Downloads/libtv_results/. You can specify a custom directory using the --output-dir flag.

faq.question: Can I continue a previous generation session?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --session-id flag with create_session.py to send new messages to an existing session.

faq.question: What models are available for generation?
faq.answer: The skill supports Seedance 2.0, Kling 3.0/O3, Wan 2.6, NanoBanana, Midjourney, Seedream 5.0, and other models available on liblib.tv.
